mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 3.5% indicates a tightening labor market, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE declines below 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate signal monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances with concurrent CPIAUCSL > 250 and FEDFUNDS > 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) combined with rising consumer spending (RSAFS) indicate robust demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in 50%+ of instances with concurrent HOUST > 1350 and RSAFS > 525000.

mechanism_narrative: Strong job growth (PAYEMS) signals corporate capacity for wage increases and consumption, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 40%+ of instances with PAYEMS growth > 150k.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in housing permits (HOUST) above 1400 signals strong demand for construction, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 50%+ of instances with HOUST > 1400.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and declining GDP (GDPC1) indicates supply-side bottlenecks, creating inflationary pressures that drive negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in 50%+ of instances with INDPRO growth > 1% and GDPC1 decline > 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS > 4%) combined with high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 260) creates a hostile environment for equities, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances with FEDFUNDS > 4% and CPIAUCSL > 260.
